sections.content: The LEFT action caused a board change. Looking at the new image, the gray frame with the purple block inside seems to have rotated or changed shape - it now looks like a diamond shape. Let me inspect the segmentation to understand what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# LEFT caused a board change - the purple block in the gray frame changed shape
# Let me compare before and after
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

print("=== BEFORE LEFT ===")
before_seg = before.segmentation
for node in before_seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  Node {node['id']}: color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")

print("\n=== AFTER LEFT ===")
after_seg = after.segmentation
for node in after_seg['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  Node {node['id']}: color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what happened with LEFT:
- Node 11 (gray border): was rows [24-32], cols [25-38], pixels=30 → now rows [21-37], cols [14-30], pixels=43
- Node 12 (purple inside): was rows [25-31], cols [26-37], pixels=84 → now rows [23-35], cols [16-28], pixels=85
The purple block with gray border moved and rotated. It seems like LEFT rotates the selected object counterclockwise. The object also moved position.
Let me understand the relationship:
- Before: gray border was a rectangle (rows 24-32, cols 25-38) = 9x14, purple inside was 7x12
- After: gray border is now rotated (rows 21-37, cols 14-30) = 17x17, purple inside is 13x13
The shape changed from a rectangle to a diamond/rotated shape. This is consistent with a 90-degree rotation.
Let me try RIGHT to see if it rotates the other way, and try UP/DOWN to understand the full control scheme.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# LEFT rotated the purple block with gray border counterclockwise
# Let me try RIGHT to rotate back, then explore UP/DOWN

result = action(['RIGHT'])
print("After RIGHT:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['id'] >= 11:
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Node {node['id']}: color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
